skill.name: sparc-methodology
skill.description: SPARC methodology guide for structured software development with specification, architecture, TDD refinement, review, and completion phases.
skill.summary: Guide structured development with SPARC phases, Claude Flow modes, orchestration patterns, and TDD workflows.
skill.author: Claude Flow

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity weak cryptography and Ruby backtick findings, but review found these are false positives caused by SPARC text, Markdown inline code, and fenced examples. The real risk is moderate: the skill documents npx and local CLI commands for claude-flow, including alpha package usage, which can execute external package code if users run them.

medium_findings.title: External Package Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill provides npx and local CLI command examples for claude-flow, including claude-flow@alpha. This appears to support the documented workflow, but running remote package commands can execute third-party code and should be reviewed by users before execution. Static Ruby backtick alerts at nearby lines are false positives from Markdown formatting, not Ruby execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly visible and use npx or a local executable. The intent appears instructional rather than malicious, so the concern is supply-chain and execution risk rather than confirmed compromise.

low_findings.title: External Resource Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to GitHub documentation, GitHub issues, and an npm package page. These links are normal support references, but they direct users outside the marketplace and should remain visible to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are present at the cited lines and are ordinary project resources. No evidence found that the links receive secrets or perform data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote CLI Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation recommends npx commands that may download and execute package code. This is a legitimate activation path for the workflow, but it creates a user-controlled execution boundary that warrants a marketplace warning.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and executable if copied by a user. No evidence found of hidden execution, credential theft, obfuscation, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Complex software work can lose structure when planning, testing, and review happen out of order. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through SPARC phases with orchestration, TDD, and review practices.
actual_capabilities: Explains the SPARC phases from specification through completion.
Lists specialized Claude Flow modes for planning, coding, testing, review, and documentation.
Provides MCP and CLI activation examples for SPARC workflows.
Describes orchestration patterns for sequential, parallel, and swarm-based work.
Documents TDD workflows with red, green, refactor, review, and optimization steps.
Includes best practices for memory use, hooks, coverage targets, and performance monitoring.
limitations: It is a Markdown guide, not an installed automation runtime by itself.
CLI examples require claude-flow or npx access in the user environment.
It assumes users understand Claude Flow MCP tools and multi-agent concepts.
It does not verify command safety or package integrity before execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Use SPARC phases to define requirements, choose architecture, coordinate implementation, and require tests before completion.

use_cases.title: Run Test-Driven Implementation
use_cases.description: Use the TDD mode guidance to write failing tests, implement the smallest change, refactor, and review coverage.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Work
use_cases.description: Use orchestration examples to assign research, architecture, coding, testing, and documentation work across agents.

prompt_templates.title: Start a SPARC Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SPARC methodology to plan this feature: [feature]. Start with requirements, constraints, success criteria, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Before Coding
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the SPARC architecture phase to [system]. Define components, interfaces, data flow, and test boundaries before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Drive TDD Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SPARC refinement for [feature]. Create the red, green, and refactor plan, including test cases and coverage expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Orchestrate Full Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use SPARC with multi-agent orchestration for [project]. Assign research, architecture, coding, testing, review, optimization, and documentation tasks.

output: Specification with roles, login methods, session rules, and security constraints.
Architecture outline for identity storage, token handling, middleware, and audit logging.
TDD refinement plan with failing tests, minimal implementation steps, and review checks.
Analyzer tasks for profiling endpoints and identifying bottlenecks.
Optimizer tasks for caching, query tuning, and response time improvements.
Tester and reviewer tasks for benchmarks, regressions, and final validation.

output_examples.input: Use SPARC to refactor a legacy module.
output: Research and analysis summary for dependencies and current behavior.
Architecture plan for incremental replacement with stable interfaces.
Completion checklist covering tests, documentation, and knowledge capture.

best_practices: Define requirements, constraints, and success criteria before requesting implementation.
Use TDD prompts that name the expected failing tests and coverage target.
Review command examples before running npx or local CLI commands from the guide.
anti_patterns: Skipping specification and asking for implementation before architecture is clear.
Using many agents without assigning clear responsibilities and shared context.
Running remote CLI commands without reviewing the package source and version.

faq.question: What is SPARC?
faq.answer: SPARC means Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, and Completion. It is a structured method for software delivery.

faq.question: Does this skill write code automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides workflow guidance and examples. Code generation depends on the connected Claude Flow tools or the assistant using the guidance.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes Claude Code and MCP-style examples for coordinating Claude Flow SPARC modes.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex can apply the SPARC phases as a planning and execution framework for coding tasks.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The guide includes npx command examples. Running remote package commands can execute third-party code in your environment.

faq.question: Is malicious behavior confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The review found no prompt injection, secret exfiltration, obfuscation, or hidden executable files in the scanned skill.
